footer.disclosureBody: {{brand}} is a licensed mortgage lender. {{nmls}}. All loans are subject to credit approval, underwriting guidelines, and property qualification. Rates, terms, and fees are subject to change without notice. Programs, rates, and conditions are subject to change without notice. Not a commitment to lend. Equal Housing Lender. State licenses available upon request.
footer.tcpaBody: By submitting your information, you consent to be contacted by {{brand}} via phone, SMS, or email about loan products. Message and data rates may apply. Consent is not a condition of service. See our Privacy Policy for details on TCPA compliance.

about.metaDesc: Ensure Home Loans (NMLS #1666674) — your one stop shop for home loans. 250+ MLO partners across 29+ states, backed by 40+ reputable lenders.
about.intro: Ensure Home Loans is your one stop shop for home loans. We partner with 250+ licensed Mortgage Loan Originators across 29+ states, backed by 40+ reputable lender relationships — so every borrower gets the right loan at the right price, the first time.
about.differentTitle: A different kind of mortgage company
about.mloFirstBody: We don't employ — we partner with MLOs. That means more flexibility, better pricing, and personal service.
about.everyLoanBody: Conventional, Non-QM, Jumbo, DSCR, Bank Statement, Hard Money, Construction, Commercial — you name it.
about.lenderNetBody: We shop your loan across our entire network so you always get the sharpest rate and structure.
about.youName: You name it, and we will get it for you.
about.licensingBody: {{brand}} is a state-licensed mortgage lender. {{nmls}}. Verify our license and view all state authorizations at the

affordability.metaTitle: How Much House Can I Afford? — Ensure Home Loans
affordability.metaDesc: Free affordability calculator. See your maximum home price based on income, debts, and down payment.
affordability.title: How Much House Can I Afford?
affordability.subtitle: Based on the 28/36 debt-to-income rule used by most US lenders.
affordability.debts: Monthly debt payments (cars, student loans, cards)
affordability.canAfford: You can afford a home up to
affordability.monthlyPay: With a monthly housing payment of about {{amt}}
affordability.calcBody: We use the 36% back-end DTI ratio (total monthly debts including housing should not exceed 36% of gross income). Property tax is estimated at 1.1% of home value, insurance at 0.4%. Your actual rates and qualification depend on credit, location, and full underwriting.

refi.metaDesc: Should you refinance? See your new monthly payment, breakeven point, and lifetime savings.
refi.title: Refinance Calculator
refi.subtitle: See if refinancing makes sense — including breakeven and lifetime savings.
refi.worthIt: ✓ Refinancing looks worthwhile — you'll recoup closing costs in about {{n}} months.
refi.longTerm: Breakeven is over 5 years — only worthwhile if you'll stay in the home long-term.
refi.noLower: New rate doesn't lower your payment with this term.

faq.metaDesc: Answers to common questions about mortgages, rates, pre-qualification, closing costs, and the loan process.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to the questions we hear most.
faq.stillBody: Talk to a licensed loan officer — no pressure, no obligation.

qa.preQualVsApprove: What's the difference between pre-qualified and pre-approved?
qa.preQualVsApproveA: Pre-qualification is a quick estimate based on the info you share. Pre-approval is a verified review of your credit, income, and assets — sellers take it much more seriously.
qa.downPayment: How much down payment do I need?
qa.downPaymentA: As little as 0% with VA or USDA, 3% with conventional, or 3.5% with FHA. We'll show you every option side by side.
qa.creditCheck: Will checking rates hurt my credit?
qa.creditCheckA: No. Our pre-qualification uses a soft pull — no impact to your score.
qa.howLong: How long does it take to close?
qa.howLongA: Most purchase loans close in 21–30 days. Refinances typically take 30–45 days.
qa.rateFactorsA: Credit score, loan-to-value, loan type, property type, occupancy, and current market conditions all factor in.
qa.closingCostsA: Closing costs usually run 2–5% of the loan amount. We provide a Loan Estimate within 3 business days of application.
qa.rateLockA: Yes — we offer 30, 45, 60, and 90-day rate locks, plus float-down options on most programs.
qa.lenderFeesA: Our pricing is transparent and competitive. You'll see every fee on your Loan Estimate before committing.
qa.docsA: Typically: 2 years W-2s or tax returns, 30 days of pay stubs, 2 months of bank statements, and ID. Self-employed borrowers may need additional business documents.
qa.agentFirst: Do I need a real estate agent first?
qa.agentFirstA: Not at all. Many buyers get pre-approved first to know their budget, then shop.
qa.giftFunds: Can I use gift funds for my down payment?
qa.giftFundsA: Yes — most loan programs allow gift funds from family members with a simple gift letter.
qa.refiWhenA: Generally when you can lower your rate by 0.5–0.75%, shorten your term, eliminate PMI, or tap equity for a major need.
qa.cashOutA: It replaces your current loan with a larger one and gives you the difference in cash — useful for renovations or debt consolidation.
